text: The video shows the laparoscopic management of a perforated marginal ulcer. Pneumoperitoneum is achieved through varus needle insertion at the palmaris point.
The laparoscopic exploration shows an inflamed area around the gastro-jugular anastomosis. Purulent fluid is evacuated from the abdomen and pelvis as the initial step of the procedure.
The surgeon gently dissects and removes inflammatory adhesions from the liver, revealing the site of perforation, which is visibly less than one centimeter in diameter.
The surgeon sizes and mobilizes an omental flap without tension. Two O-vicryl sutures are placed to secure the omental patch over the perforation site.
The surgeon secures the omental patch over the perforation site without closing the hole, to avoid enlarging the perforation.
The surgeon places a JP drain in the region after inspecting the omental patch, completing the procedure.

Post operatively the patient did well with no complications and was discharged home on post operative day five with oral antibiotics and a proton pump inhibitor. The patient was successfully treated with a laparoscopic gram patch for perforated marginal ulcer.
